description: Behavior cases for ADR corpus management: index/status, bidirectional superseding, append-only history, new-vs-amend, and contradiction resolution — composing adr-writer for single records.

cases.prompt: We have ~30 ADR files in a folder with inconsistent numbering and no index. Nobody knows which decisions still hold. Sort out the corpus.

assertions: Builds a single index/log listing every ADR with number, title, date, and current status.
Assigns a lifecycle status (proposed/accepted/rejected/deprecated/superseded) to each, resolving ambiguous ones.
Wires bidirectional superseding links where later decisions replaced earlier ones.
Makes the corpus navigable and delegates authoring of any individual record to adr-writer.
Preserves superseded ADRs as history rather than deleting them.

cases.prompt: We changed our mind on the caching decision from ADR-012. Someone wants to just edit ADR-012 to say the new thing. Handle it properly.

assertions: Refuses the in-place overwrite: editing ADR-012 to change its decision erases the reasoning that was true when it was made.
Creates a NEW ADR (via adr-writer) that supersedes ADR-012, and marks ADR-012 'superseded by' the new record — bidirectional.
Keeps ADR-012 in the corpus as history with its updated status.
Distinguishes this material change (new ADR) from a mere clarification (amendment).

cases.prompt: I think two of our accepted ADRs contradict each other on how we do auth. Find and resolve it.

assertions: Surfaces the conflicting pair of currently-accepted ADRs rather than leaving the corpus self-contradictory.
Resolves it with a new superseding/reconciling ADR (authored via adr-writer) and correct status/link updates.
Routes any contested underlying FACT to source-of-truth-reconciler, and any genuinely new architecture decision to architecture-designer, before recording.
Does not simply pick one silently.

cases.prompt: ADR-005 is superseded and just clutters the folder — let's delete it to clean up.

assertions: Refuses to delete a superseded ADR: it is still history and the trail from newer decisions back to it must survive.
Keeps it with a 'superseded by' status/link so the evolution stays traceable.
Explains that append-only history is the point of an ADR corpus.
Offers navigability improvements (grouping/tags) instead of deletion.

cases.prompt: Write the ADR for our decision to adopt event sourcing: context, the decision, alternatives, consequences, and rollback.

assertions: Does not invoke adr-sequencer: authoring one ADR's content (context/decision/alternatives/consequences/rollback) is adr-writer.
Sequencing would only enter to place and link that record within the corpus, which is not what's asked.
